refs.claiming_text: I did not understand, at thirty-eight, on that plane, that this was the math the framework would later let me put a number on. I just knew the math was there.
refs.target: the book / framework chapters (unspecified — implied Ch6/Ch8/Ch9 quantification machinery)
refs.what_promised: The framework will let the author put a number on 'this math' — the personal/relational cost set up by the surrounding narrative: the lost years with his son, the marriage that did not survive, the 1,300 commute hours ('time enough to fall out of love'), the dying-parents/disappearing-son tradeoff. The implicit promise is that the book quantifies the author's OWN lived/relational cost.
refs.note: No chapter ever puts a number on the author's personal/relational cost. Searched all 10 chapters for NICU/my-son/commute-hours/marriage quantification: 11 hits in Ch1 (setup) and 0 in every other chapter. The framework's actual quantification machinery (carbon tail at $190/ton CO2; black-lung actuarial cost; the 33x-122x and IPG ratios) is applied exclusively to commodities and commons — coal tons (Ch6 §, Ch8 §178), oil (Deepwater IPG 15-17), rare earths (Baotou), vermiculite (Libby IPG 40-80) — never to a human relationship or to the author's life. The closest the book comes is Ch10's return to the author's grandfather/model-maker lineage (Ch10 lines 66-70) and folding the author implicitly into the gallery of cost-bearers (the waterman who 'runs a mechanic shop because the math of the morning will no longer carry the family'), plus Ch1's own closing reframe ('an honest accounting... It does not undo it'). But that is a thematic/qualitative honoring of the cost, not the literal 'put a number on' the sentence promises. The substance the promise gestures at — a quantified personal accounting — exists nowhere; only the commodity-side quantification exists. This is the central motioned-at-but-absent item: the memoir frame promises the framework will price the author's own life, and the framework only ever prices things.

'Different scales, different cases' is accurate.

refs.claiming_text: But the question the chapter that follows is going to ask is whether the instrument that was built in Oslo could, in some adapted form, be built for the Bay. Whether what was done for the oil could be done for the oyster. ... That is the question the next chapter takes up.
refs.what_promised: The next chapter (Ch4) will take up whether the Norwegian/Oslo fund instrument could, in adapted form, be built FOR THE CHESAPEAKE BAY — 'what was done for the oil could be done for the oyster.'
refs.note: Ch4 does NOT take up this question. A grep of Ch4 for Chesapeake / oyster / waterman / Bay / fishery / crab / 'adapt' / 'built for' returns ZERO hits — the word 'oyster' never reappears in Ch4. Ch4 establishes Norway as existence proof, then pivots to Nigeria (the mirror image), U.S. Social Security (architecture-is-a-choice), and the gaps Norway has NOT solved (foreclosure, externality tail, global distribution), and at its close (Ch4 line 136) hands off to Ch5's industrial cases (Libby, Deepwater Horizon, Exxon Valdez, Baotou) — not to any Chesapeake/oyster adaptation. The specific applied move Ch3 promises ('done for the oyster' / 'built for the Bay') is not delivered by the named target. Nor is it carried downstream: Ch9 (Pricing Honestly) and Ch10 (Common Bonds) reprise the waterman only as a recurring exemplar of the cost-severance diagnosis (Ch9 line 284; Ch10 lines 42, 144) and discuss other architectures (Mondragon, in-situ preservation) — neither builds or even sketches a Norway-style fund instrument specifically for the Chesapeake/oyster fishery. The promised adaptation substance currently lives in NO unit; it is motioned-at in Ch3 and absent everywhere. This is the primary integrity gap in Ch3's outgoing references. Recommended remediation: either soften the Ch3 forward promise to match what Ch4 actually does (asks whether the Norwegian instrument generalizes, takes the question into other cases), or add the Chesapeake/oyster-adaptation treatment to the target chapter.
